Package org.rodinp.core

Interface IRodinProject

All Superinterfaces:
org.eclipse.core.runtime.IAdaptable, IOpenable, IParent, IRodinElement
All Known Implementing Classes:
RodinProject
public interface IRodinProject extends IParent, IRodinElement, IOpenable
A Rodin project represents a view of a project resource in terms of Rodin elements. A project may contain several Rodin files.

Rodin project elements need to be opened before they can be navigated or manipulated. The children of a Rodin project are the Rodin files that are contained in this project.

Note that a resource project which is closed in the workspace (using for instance IProject.close(IProgressMonitor)) doesn't correspond anymore to a Rodin project, even if it has the Rodin nature. It is therefore considered as a non-Rodin resource project and not part of the database.

An instance of one of these handles can be created via RodinCore.create(project).

Since:
1.0
See Also:
  • RodinCore.valueOf(IProject)

    Returns the IProject on which this IRodinProject was created.

    Returns the root elements of the Rodin files of this project that are of the given element type.

    Returns whether this project has been built at least once and thus whether it has a build state.

  • Field Details

    • ELEMENT_TYPE

      static final IElementType<IRodinProject> ELEMENT_TYPE
      The element type of all Rodin projects.

  • Method Details

    • getNonRodinResources

      org.eclipse.core.resources.IResource[] getNonRodinResources() throws RodinDBException
      Returns an array of non-Rodin resources directly contained in this project. It does not transitively answer non-Rodin resources contained in folders; these would have to be explicitly iterated over.

      Non-Rodin resources includes other files and folders located in the project.

      Returns:
      an array of non-Rodin resources (IFiles and/or IFolders) directly contained in this project
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element does not exist or if an exception occurs while accessing its corresponding resource

    • getProject

      org.eclipse.core.resources.IProject getProject()
      Returns the IProject on which this IRodinProject was created. This is a handle-only method.
      Returns:
      the IProject on which this IRodinProject was created

    • getRodinFile

      IRodinFile getRodinFile(String fileName)
      Returns a handle to the primary copy of the Rodin file with the specified name in this project (for example, "toto.mdl"). The name must be a valid Rodin file name, or null will be returned.

      This is a handle-only method. The Rodin file may or may not be present.

      Parameters:
      fileName - the name of the Rodin file
      Returns:
      the Rodin file with the specified name in this project or null if the given file name doesn't correspond to a Rodin file name (for instance, wrong extension).

    • hasBuildState

      boolean hasBuildState()
      Returns whether this project has been built at least once and thus whether it has a build state.
      Returns:
      true if this project has been built at least once, false otherwise

    • getRodinFiles

      IRodinFile[] getRodinFiles() throws RodinDBException
      Returns all the Rodin files in this project.
      Returns:
      all of the Rodin files in this project
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element does not exist or if an exception occurs while accessing its corresponding resource.

    • getRootElementsOfType

      <T extends IInternalElement> T[] getRootElementsOfType(IInternalElementType<T> type) throws RodinDBException
      Returns the root elements of the Rodin files of this project that are of the given element type.
      Parameters:
      type - type of the root elements to retrieve
      Returns:
      the root elements of this project that are of the given type
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this project does not exist, or if an exception occurs while accessing its children files
